title: feat(audio): support OpenAI diarized transcriptions
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds `response_format=diarized_json` on `/v1/audio/transcriptions` for diarized STT responses.
- Maps OpenAI-style diarization internally to AssemblyAI `speaker_labels` and ElevenLabs Scribe `diarize`.
- Returns OpenAI-compatible `segments[]` with speaker, text, start, and end.
- Keeps `speakers_expected` as an optional provider hint for `diarized_json`.
- Defers provider-specific speaker-count and model-support errors to the selected upstream.

gist.headline: Who said what?
gist.blurb: Added speaker diarization to our audio transcription API. Now you can easily tell exactly which bee is buzzing in your audio files.
gist.summary: Added `response_format=diarized_json` to the `/v1/audio/transcriptions` endpoint, returning OpenAI-compatible segments with speaker labels powered by AssemblyAI and ElevenLabs Scribe.
gist.impact: Developers can now automatically identify different speakers in audio files, receiving structured JSON with text, start/end times, and speaker IDs.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot (Polly) wearing headphones, listening to a conversation between the monitor robot and the nomnom creature, taking notes to track who is speaking. Represents speaker diarization (identifying who said what).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny retro headphones, sitting at a glowing lime green (#ecf874) terminal and taking notes on a clipboard. Nearby, a chunky monitor robot and a round nomnom creature are having a conversation, with little pixel speech bubbles above them. Soft pastel lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley, emotionally warm.
